Empires & Puzzles Goseck Review: The Insidious Gargoyle Strategy Guide

Goseck plays a dangerous game: the closer he is to death, the harder he hits. With a potential 700% damage to all enemies, he was once the ultimate nuclear option. But in a world of 3000 HP titans, is his Stone Skin enough to keep his glass jaw intact? We dive into the math of misery.
Goseck is the embodiment of "high risk, high reward." Released back in 2022 as part of the Sanctuary of Gargoyles family, this
Legendary hero introduced a terrifying mechanic: damage that scales based on how close he is to death. He is a
mana hero who wants to take a beating, survive with a sliver of health, and then wipe the entire enemy team off the map. He is the ultimate "Call an ambulance... but not for me!" hero.

Goseck looks exactly like what you'd expect from a gargoyle who crawled out of a heavy metal album cover. He's got the classic stony skin, jagged wings, and a grin that says "I'm going to enjoy this way more than you are." The purple energy crackling around his weapon hints at the devastating power he holds back until the very last moment. It’s a solid design that fits the "Insidious" moniker perfectly—he looks sturdy, but he's actually waiting for you to crack his shell.

His base attack of ~824 (and even ~1016 at LB2) is now considered "Average 4-Star" level compared to modern 5-stars who regularly exceed 1300-1600 attack.
His durability is the real concern. 1798 HP at LB2 is shockingly low in an era where heroes walk around with 2800+ HP base.
This stat gap is critical because his entire gimmick revolves on him
at low health. With such a small HP pool, the line between "optimal damage" and "dead" is microscopic.
This skill is the reason Goseck exists. It’s a simple, brutal equation:

Back in 2022, 700% damage to all enemies was an "End Game" button. If Goseck fired at low HP, the match was over.
Today, the math is less favorable.
Because his Attack stat is so low compared to modern heroes, his "700%" isn't as scary as it sounds.

A modern fast sniper hits for ~8,000 raw damage to a single target. Goseck spreads that across 5, but the individual impact is now survivable for modern tanks.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Gargoyle family brings one of the most annoying defensive mechanics in the game:

The family bonus gives resistance to Poison (1 hero), Burn (2 heroes), and Water/Frost (3 heroes). Since Goseck is usually played solo or maybe with one other (like Kalo), the Poison immunity is a nice bonus but rarely a game-changer.

Upon release, Goseck was a terror in Rush Wars. He was the "Purple Alfrike" alternative—if he fired, you lost. There were calls for nerfs because his damage ceiling was so high.

Power creep has been unkind to Goseck. His stats have effectively halved his threat level. His "massive" damage is now just "good" damage, and his "tankiness" is non-existent without the Stone Skin active.
He is too slow and too fragile. He will likely die before firing, or fire for weak damage.
This is his retirement home. In Very Fast wars, he can still be devastating because he fires before the enemy can chip him down, or he takes one big hit, survives, and retaliates instantly.

If you pull Goseck early, he is a great "boss killer" for map stages. Let the boss hit him, then fire back. For raids, hide him in the corner and hope he survives. He will carry you through Gold and Platinum arenas simply by pure damage.

He is a luxury toy for Rush Wars. Do not LB2 him unless you have resources to burn and a deep love for the Gargoyles. There are far better Purple projects (like anyone from the Construct, Astral, or Goblin families) who offer more utility and damage without the condition of "almost dying."
“Goseck will be a very interesting tank... he will be a beast in VF.”
- This prediction held true for about a year. Now, he's a liability as a tank because he dies to tile damage too fast.
“I do wish he was still a fighter... imagine if he revived at 1 HP and fired.”
- The community correctly identified that the Fighter class (beta version) would have been broken. Barbarian was the "nerf" to keep him sane.
“He fired after receiving only 65 points of damage and he still dealt over 700 points of damage to my entire team.”
- A reminder of how scary he was in 2022. Today, 700 damage is a scratch.

Goseck is a relic of a different era. He reminds us of a time when 700% damage was a game-ending number. Today, he is a specialized tool for Rush Wars and a fun "what if" hero for seeing big numbers on the screen. He is the glass cannon that has become more glass than cannon. If you have him, dress him up for Rush, but don't expect him to carry your team against the modern gods of Empires & Puzzles.
A situational nuke that has gathered a bit too much dust. Keep for Rush, bench for the rest.
Is Goseck still the king of damage? We analyze his 'low HP, high impact' mechanic, his Gargoyle Stone Skin, and whether this Slow purple beast can survive the modern meta.
